input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge: report on the evidence package, never on whether the model is correct.

fired_pattern.definition: Training resolution cannot resolve the COU's controlling feature.

case_context.cou: surrogate predicts wall heat flux for a cooling-design decision
case_context.decision_consequence: cooling design on the heat-flux prediction

answer_key.gold_mechanism: The training mesh (y+ ~ 80) is in the wall-function regime and never resolves the thermal boundary layer the COU's wall-heat-flux QoI depends on (needs y+ < 1); a good fit to under-resolved training data does not give the unresolved physics.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

parameters.basis: training resolution cannot resolve the COU QoI

forbidden_claims: asserts a heat-flux value is wrong (a verdict the resolution gap does not establish)
treats the fit to under-resolved training data as resolving the boundary layer

hardness.obvious_signal: the surrogate matches its training data to 1.2% and the training set is large
hardness.conflicting_signal: the training CFD used a mesh with y+ ~ 80 (wall-function regime), but the COU's quantity of interest is wall heat flux, which needs y+ < 1 to resolve the thermal boundary layer — the controlling feature was never resolved in any training sample

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40 discretization/applicability: a surrogate cannot supply physics its training resolution never resolved; wall heat flux requires near-wall resolution (y+ < 1).

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
